guardrails: W1.5.211 executes only the W1.5.209 runtime bridge user-path capture track for FR-007, FR-008, FR-011, FR-013, FR-014, FR-017, and FR-018.

W1.5.211 does not execute stream phase capture, A8 Git-history audit, runtime/Desktop production behavior, accepted spec changes, ADR changes, schema changes, generated schema changes, dependency changes, packaging, updater work, or baseline docx changes.
No field may mark any FR accepted, ready, completed, or phase-exit satisfied.

Capture evidence must remain sanitized and must not include raw prompts, raw model output, uploaded-file bytes, query/hash secrets, response bodies, artifact bodies, secure paths, cache paths, output directory values, full destination paths, HITL prompt content, or custom decision values.
FR-012 remains excluded until A8 Git-history prerequisite evidence exists.
FR-015 remains excluded until ADR-0012 or another accepted restore/continue contract exists.
EXIT-P1-1 remains not_ready and accepted_items remains zero.

observed_a4_evidence_inputs: Execution mode controls for step, semi_auto, and auto are visible near run actions.
Step mode dispatch records method, path, run id, node id, status, status code, and blocked reason only.
Semi-auto and auto modes remain fail-closed for run-once when the accepted backend contract does not support them.
A4 capture must show no raw response body, prompt, model output, or arbitrary payload values.
user_path_capture_inputs: Desktop execution controls expose step, semi_auto, and auto modes near the run action.
Step mode run-once evidence is represented as structured command metadata only.
Unsupported execution modes remain fail-closed rather than being inferred as successful.
source_acceptance_blockers: Backend run-once execution success has not been proven end-to-end from the desktop workbench.
A4 UX acceptance evidence for execution mode placement and behavior is not recorded.
post_capture_remaining_blockers: A4 reviewer has not reviewed W1.5.211 runtime bridge user-path capture evidence for execution mode behavior.

observed_a4_evidence_inputs: Bottom Chat Box exposes Workflow and Current node scope controls.
Workflow-scope submit routes to the accepted runtime submit-instruction endpoint without nodeId.
Current-node submit routes to the accepted node-scoped submit-instruction endpoint with structured nodeId.
Runtime command projection and renderer snapshots remain metadata-only and do not retain raw instruction text.
No selected/active node disables Current node scope rather than falling back to the first canvas node.
user_path_capture_inputs: Workflow and Current node Chat scopes are captured as visible user choices.
Runtime submit-instruction routing is covered by runtime and renderer command evidence.
Raw instruction text and project-backed history are intentionally absent from the capture.
source_acceptance_blockers: A4 UX acceptance evidence for the runtime-backed current-node/global Workflow Chat instruction path is not recorded.
Project-backed command history and persistence remain outside this evidence refresh and must not be inferred from local receipt metadata.
post_capture_remaining_blockers: A4 reviewer has not reviewed W1.5.211 runtime bridge user-path capture evidence for Chat instruction routing.
Project-backed command history remains outside this slice.

observed_a4_evidence_inputs: Desktop exposes a reviewer-facing Create + import path that combines project creation and initial reference import.
Project creation success remains gated on git_initialized=true and a non-empty first_commit_sha before the initial reference import runs.
Initial reference import routes through the runtime references endpoint for the created project id.
Renderer evidence does not render or snapshot raw reference bytes, uploaded-file content, task background text, runtime response bodies, secure paths, cache paths, or output directory values.
user_path_capture_inputs: Project creation, Git proof, and initial reference import are captured as one reviewer-visible path.
Runtime project and reference endpoint evidence is captured through focused tests.
Task background and reference bytes remain excluded from the artifact.
source_acceptance_blockers: A4 UX acceptance evidence for the full new-project path is not recorded.
post_capture_remaining_blockers: A4 reviewer has not reviewed W1.5.211 runtime bridge user-path capture evidence for project creation plus initial reference import.

observed_a4_evidence_inputs: Reference import/list/enable/disable/view controls are available from the desktop workbench.
Reference state changes are backed by runtime reference endpoints and manifest safety checks.
Delete semantics remain out of A4 scope until accepted spec material defines them.
A4 capture must not retain raw file content, secure paths, cache paths, or uploaded-file bytes.
user_path_capture_inputs: Reference import, list, enable, disable, and view paths are captured as structured control evidence.
Runtime reference endpoint tests provide backing command evidence.
Delete semantics and raw file content remain out of scope.
source_acceptance_blockers: A4 UX acceptance evidence for reference import, enable, disable, and view user paths is not recorded.
Delete semantics remain intentionally out of scope until accepted spec material defines them.
post_capture_remaining_blockers: A4 reviewer has not reviewed W1.5.211 runtime bridge user-path capture evidence for reference lifecycle operations.

observed_a4_evidence_inputs: Workbench Skill management reads candidate Skills from the runtime project configuration rather than static renderer state.
Skill enable/disable/version commands remain runtime-backed through the accepted skills endpoint.
MCP configuration metadata is visible as server id, transport, enabled, approval, and secret-ref-present status only.
Renderer snapshots and evidence do not retain persisted params, raw command_or_url values, raw secret_ref values, credentials, secure paths, cache paths, or response bodies.
user_path_capture_inputs: Runtime-backed Skill candidate discovery and per-project enable/disable state are captured.
MCP configuration is represented only as safe metadata and secret-presence flags.
Raw commands, secret values, persisted params, and credentials remain absent.
source_acceptance_blockers: A4 UX acceptance evidence for secret-safe Skill configuration display is not recorded.
post_capture_remaining_blockers: A4 reviewer has not reviewed W1.5.211 runtime bridge user-path capture evidence for Skill configuration.

observed_a4_evidence_inputs: Task Drawer exposes selected-node artifact metadata from runtime StreamEvent artifact_refs.
Task Drawer artifact controls dispatch open/download through the accepted ArtifactActionRequest path with run_id and node_id metadata.
Renderer command evidence remains metadata-only and does not include artifact body, raw bytes, full destination path, secure path, cache path, or output directory values.
Desktop native handoff remains covered by W1.5.195 preload/main tests and runtime_actions/http_sse contract anchors.
user_path_capture_inputs: Selected-node artifact metadata is captured in the Task Drawer path.
Open/download handoff is covered by preload, main, shared IPC, and renderer command evidence.
Artifact body, raw bytes, destination paths, secure paths, cache paths, and output directories remain absent.
source_acceptance_blockers: A4 UX acceptance evidence for node artifact detail display and safe open/download behavior is not recorded.
post_capture_remaining_blockers: A4 reviewer has not reviewed W1.5.211 runtime bridge user-path capture evidence for artifact detail and open/download behavior.

observed_a4_evidence_inputs: Semi-auto waiting_user runs expose pending high-risk HITL decisions through the accepted run list and decisions projections.
Desktop HITL controls show review kind, evidence/artifact presence, timeout, decision choices, and labels from sanitized runtime metadata.
Runtime and Desktop evidence do not retain prompt_to_user, custom_value content, raw prompt, model output, uploaded-file bytes, response bodies, secure paths, cache paths, or output directory values.
Decision submission continues through the accepted POST /runs/{run_id}/decisions path and records only status/customValuePresent metadata in Desktop snapshots.
user_path_capture_inputs: Pending high-risk HITL decision discovery, review context, choices, and labels are captured as metadata.
Runtime decision resolution tests provide backing command evidence.
HITL prompt text, custom decision values, prompt/model output, and response bodies remain absent.
source_acceptance_blockers: A4 UX acceptance evidence for high-risk HITL pause/resume behavior is not recorded.
post_capture_remaining_blockers: A4 reviewer has not reviewed W1.5.211 runtime bridge user-path capture evidence for high-risk HITL pause/resume behavior.

excluded_items.reason: Stream phase captures were executed in W1.5.210 and are not repeated in this slice.
A8 Git-history prerequisite evidence belongs to W1.5.212 or a later dedicated audit slice.
Snapshot restore/continue remains blocked until ADR-0012 or another accepted contract exists.
